2603.16697.title: Cost Trade-offs in Matrix Inversion Updates for Streaming Outlier Detection
2603.16697.paper_summary: Outlier detection identifies data points that deviate significantly from expected patterns, revealing anomalies that may require special attention. Incorporating online learning further improves accuracy by continuously updating the model to reflect the most recent data. When employing the Christoffel function as an outlier score, online learning requires updating the inverse of a matrix following a rank-k update, given the initial inverse. Surprisingly, there is no consensus on the optimal method for this task. This technical note aims to compare three different updating methods: Direct Inversion (DI), Iterative Sherman-Morrison (ISM), and Woodbury Matrix Identity (WMI), to identify the most suitable approach for different scenarios. We first derive the theoretical computational costs of each method and then validate these findings through comprehensive Python simulations run on a CPU. These results allow us to propose a simple, quantitative, and easy-to-remember rule that can be stated qualitatively as follows: ISM is optimal for rank-1 updates, WMI excels for small updates relative to matrix size, and DI is preferable otherwise. This technical note produces a general result for any problem involving a matrix inversion update. In particular, it contributes to the ongoing development of efficient online outlier detection techniques.
2603.16697.paper_summary_zh: 異常值檢測識別出顯著偏離預期模式的數據點，揭示可能需要特別關注的異常情況。<lb/>結合在線學習進一步提高準確性，通過不斷更新模型以反映最新數據。<lb/>在使用Christoffel函數作為異常值分數時，在線學習需要在給定初始逆矩陣的情況下，根據秩-k更新來更新矩陣的逆。<lb/>令人驚訝的是，對於這項任務的最佳方法並沒有共識。<lb/>這份技術備忘錄旨在比較三種不同的更新方法：直接反演（DI）、迭代Sherman-Morrison（ISM）和Woodbury矩陣恒等式（WMI），以確定最適合不同場景的方法。<lb/>我們首先推導出每種方法的理論計算成本，然後通過在CPU上運行的全面Python模擬來驗證這些發現。<lb/>這些結果使我們能夠提出一個簡單、定量且易於記憶的規則，該規則可以定性地表述為：ISM對於秩-1更新是最佳的，WMI在相對於矩陣大小的小更新中表現優越，而DI在其他情況下更可取。<lb/>這份技術備忘錄為涉及矩陣反演更新的任何問題產生了一個一般結果。<lb/>特別地，它對高效在線異常值檢測技術的持續發展作出了貢獻。
2603.16697.author: Florian Grivet et.al.
